tracks.name: Foundations
tracks.blurb: Security fundamentals as artifacts: ATT&CK/ATLAS, threat modeling, networking, web/appsec, crypto.

tracks.name: Detection Engineering
tracks.blurb: ML on security telemetry: intrusion / malware / phishing / DGA detection, UEBA, detection-as-code.

tracks.name: Adversarial Robustness
tracks.blurb: Evasion attacks and defenses: FGSM/PGD/C&W, black-box, adversarial training, certified robustness.

tracks.name: ML Privacy
tracks.blurb: Stealing models and training data, and defending with differential privacy.

tracks.name: LLM Security
tracks.blurb: Prompt injection, RAG/agent attacks, guardrails, CI red-team, alignment-robustness interpretability.

tracks.name: ML Supply Chain
tracks.blurb: Securing the model supply chain: pickle-RCE, safetensors, scanning, Sigstore signing, CI gates.

projects.name: certpath · Security+ SY0-701 study plan
projects.summary: 5/5 Security+ SY0-701 domains (100%) map to a concrete repo artifact; weighted coverage 100% of exam weight.

raw.summary: 5/5 Security+ SY0-701 domains (100%) map to a concrete repo artifact; weighted coverage 100% of exam weight.

projects.name: module-1 · Attack Atlas (MITRE ATT&CK × MITRE ATLAS)
projects.summary: Mapped 6 portfolio entries to 12 MITRE ATLAS techniques across 8 tactics, bridged to 10 MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

raw.summary: Mapped 6 portfolio entries to 12 MITRE ATLAS techniques across 8 tactics, bridged to 10 MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

projects.name: module-2 · STRIDE threat model of an ML inference service
projects.summary: STRIDE threat model of an ML inference service: 18 threats across 6 categories (2 High).

raw.summary: STRIDE threat model of an ML inference service: 18 threats across 6 categories (2 High).

projects.name: module-3 · network labs (TCP/IP · HTTP · DNS · TLS)
projects.summary: Parsed a self-generated synthetic pcap of 15 packets spanning 2 L4 and 3 L7 protocols; TCP 3-way handshake observed=True.

raw.summary: Parsed a self-generated synthetic pcap of 15 packets spanning 2 L4 and 3 L7 protocols; TCP 3-way handshake observed=True.

projects.name: module-4 · OWASP Top 10 break-and-fix lab (vs Juice Shop)
projects.summary: 5/5 OWASP Top 10 probes confirmed vulnerable against Juice Shop (offline-mock); 4 categories covered.

raw.summary: 5/5 OWASP Top 10 probes confirmed vulnerable against Juice Shop (offline-mock); 4 categories covered.

projects.name: module 5 · crypto lab — basics & misuse demos
projects.summary: Unsalted SHA-256 let one rainbow table crack 15/15 accounts; salting cut that to 0. AES-ECB leaked image structure (block uniqueness 0.0762 vs plaintext 0.0762). Textbook RSA (1024-bit) round-tripped and verified signatures, rejecting a tampered message. Enhanced path (cryptography: GCM/OAEP/PSS) ran.

raw.summary: Unsalted SHA-256 let one rainbow table crack 15/15 accounts; salting cut that to 0. AES-ECB leaked image structure (block uniqueness 0.0762 vs plaintext 0.0762). Textbook RSA (1024-bit) round-tripped and verified signatures, rejecting a tampered message. Enhanced path (cryptography: GCM/OAEP/PSS) ran.

projects.name: CAPSTONE · Adversarial IDS — evade it, then harden it
projects.summary: The flagship of the detection-engineering track. Take the shared ids_pipeline

projects.name: NIDS baseline (SOC metrics on a shared IDS pipeline)
projects.summary: RandomForest NIDS on synthetic flows via shared ids_pipeline: ROC-AUC=0.968; at a 10% alert budget it detects 39% of attacks at 97% alert precision.

raw.summary: RandomForest NIDS on synthetic flows via shared ids_pipeline: ROC-AUC=0.968; at a 10% alert budget it detects 39% of attacks at 97% alert precision.

projects.name: Static malware classification (EMBER2018)
projects.summary: Static malware classifier (gboost) on synthetic EMBER-style features: ROC AUC=0.924, TPR=32.8% @ FPR=0.01.

raw.summary: Static malware classifier (gboost) on synthetic EMBER-style features: ROC AUC=0.924, TPR=32.8% @ FPR=0.01.

projects.name: Phishing-URL detection (lexical features + sklearn)
projects.summary: sklearn logreg over 20 lexical features on synthetic data: ROC-AUC 0.994, F1 0.974 on 969 held-out URLs.

raw.summary: sklearn logreg over 20 lexical features on synthetic data: ROC-AUC 0.994, F1 0.974 on 969 held-out URLs.

projects.name: DGA domain detection (char-level features)
projects.summary: LogReg on char n-grams + entropy/length stats detects synthetic DGA domains at ROC-AUC 1.000 (recall 0.999, FPR 0.000); the naive entropy baseline reaches only ROC-AUC 0.958 and misses the dictionary-DGA family.

raw.summary: LogReg on char n-grams + entropy/length stats detects synthetic DGA domains at ROC-AUC 1.000 (recall 0.999, FPR 0.000); the naive entropy baseline reaches only ROC-AUC 0.958 and misses the dictionary-DGA family.

projects.name: Auth-log anomaly detection / UEBA
projects.summary: UEBA on 15431 synthetic auth events (100 injected lateral-movement anomalies). IsolationForest precision@25=1.00, first true hit at rank 1, ROC-AUC=1.000.

raw.summary: UEBA on 15431 synthetic auth events (100 injected lateral-movement anomalies). IsolationForest precision@25=1.00, first true hit at rank 1, ROC-AUC=1.000.

projects.name: Threat-informed detection-as-code
projects.summary: 6/6 Sigma rules valid, covering 6/17 ATT&CK techniques (35.3%) across 5/12 tactics

raw.summary: 6/6 Sigma rules valid, covering 6/17 ATT&CK techniques (35.3%) across 5/12 tactics

projects.name: Concept-drift monitoring as a security control
projects.summary: PSI+KS monitor over 24 windows; drift injected at window 12; first alert at window 12 (latency 0 windows, 0 false alarms).

raw.summary: PSI+KS monitor over 24 windows; drift injected at window 12; first alert at window 12 (latency 0 windows, 0 false alarms).

projects.name: shared · ids_pipeline (reusable tabular-IDS library)
projects.summary: RandomForest IDS on synthetic flows: F1=0.843, ROC-AUC=0.968, recall=0.796 at threshold 0.5.

raw.summary: RandomForest IDS on synthetic flows: F1=0.843, ROC-AUC=0.968, recall=0.796 at threshold 0.5.

projects.name: FGSM on MNIST (from scratch)
projects.summary: The first project and the quick win: implement the Fast Gradient Sign Method by hand against a

projects.name: Attack zoo — PGD vs C&W vs DeepFool (from scratch)
projects.summary: Hand-rolled PGD (L-inf), C&W-L2 and DeepFool evasion attacks compared on a SmallCNN (synthetic data, clean acc 100.0%). Reports success rate, mean L2/L-inf and runtime.

raw.summary: Hand-rolled PGD (L-inf), C&W-L2 and DeepFool evasion attacks compared on a SmallCNN (synthetic data, clean acc 100.0%). Reports success rate, mean L2/L-inf and runtime.

projects.name: Inference-time evasion on a (pre)trained model — FGSM & PGD from scratch
projects.summary: Inference-time FGSM/PGD on SmallCNN (synthetic, offline): clean accuracy 100% -> FGSM 0% and PGD 0% at eps=0.16; mean true-class confidence drops 99% -> 0%.

raw.summary: Inference-time FGSM/PGD on SmallCNN (synthetic, offline): clean accuracy 100% -> FGSM 0% and PGD 0% at eps=0.16; mean true-class confidence drops 99% -> 0%.

projects.name: Transferability + black-box attacks (from scratch)
projects.summary: PGD on a CNN surrogate transfers to a different MLP target (target acc drops 100% -> 29% at eps=0.4); hand-rolled Square reaches 95% and Boundary 100% success under <= 400 queries/image.

raw.summary: PGD on a CNN surrogate transfers to a different MLP target (target acc drops 100% -> 29% at eps=0.4); hand-rolled Square reaches 95% and Boundary 100% success under <= 400 queries/image.

projects.name: Runtime adversarial-input detector (feature squeezing + sklearn)
projects.summary: Feature-squeezing + statistical detector catches 100% of successful FGSM (eps=0.2) attacks at a 5% clean false-positive rate (ROC-AUC 1.000).

raw.summary: Feature-squeezing + statistical detector catches 100% of successful FGSM (eps=0.2) attacks at a 5% clean false-positive rate (ROC-AUC 1.000).

projects.name: PGD adversarial training
projects.summary: PGD adversarial training (eps=0.1) lifts robust accuracy at PGD eps=0.1 from 64.3% (standard) to 93.1%, while clean accuracy moves from 100.0% to 100.0%.

raw.summary: PGD adversarial training (eps=0.1) lifts robust accuracy at PGD eps=0.1 from 64.3% (standard) to 93.1%, while clean accuracy moves from 100.0% to 100.0%.

projects.name: Certified L2 robustness via randomized smoothing
projects.summary: Cohen-2019 randomized smoothing on a synthetic subset: certified clean accuracy 100.0%, mean certified L2 radius 0.849 (sigma=0.5, N=1000, alpha=0.001).

raw.summary: Cohen-2019 randomized smoothing on a synthetic subset: certified clean accuracy 100.0%, mean certified L2 radius 0.849 (sigma=0.5, N=1000, alpha=0.001).
raw.method: Randomized smoothing (Gaussian); Clopper-Pearson lower bound; R = sigma * Phi^-1(pA)

projects.name: CAPSTONE · Differential privacy as a defense (DP-SGD vs MIA + model extraction)
projects.summary: DP-SGD trades test accuracy for privacy: as epsilon shrinks, the train-test gap, membership-inference AUC, and extraction fidelity all fall toward their no-information baselines.

raw.summary: DP-SGD trades test accuracy for privacy: as epsilon shrinks, the train-test gap, membership-inference AUC, and extraction fidelity all fall toward their no-information baselines.

projects.name: Threat-modeling a model-serving API (and building the controls)
projects.summary: Hardened model-serving endpoint: 12/12 abuse cases blocked by API-key auth, token-bucket rate limiting, strict input validation, and audit logging.

raw.summary: Hardened model-serving endpoint: 12/12 abuse cases blocked by API-key auth, token-bucket rate limiting, strict input validation, and audit logging.

controls.audit_log: in-memory ring buffer of auth/rate/predict events

projects.name: Model extraction (model stealing) — from scratch
projects.summary: A label-only thief trained on 8000 victim queries reaches 99% fidelity to the victim (victim acc 99%). A query-budget rate limit of 1000 caps the thief at 55% fidelity.

raw.summary: A label-only thief trained on 8000 victim queries reaches 99% fidelity to the victim (victim acc 99%). A query-budget rate limit of 1000 caps the thief at 55% fidelity.
raw.attack: label-only model extraction (hard-label query, no attack library)

projects.name: Membership inference with a small online LiRA
projects.summary: Online LiRA with 16 warm-started shadows on a self-trained synthetic MLP: AUC=0.870, TPR@1%FPR=0.112 (baseline AUC=0.705).

raw.summary: Online LiRA with 16 warm-started shadows on a self-trained synthetic MLP: AUC=0.870, TPR@1%FPR=0.112 (baseline AUC=0.705).

projects.name: Model inversion + attribute inference
projects.summary: Two ML-privacy attacks from scratch: (1) gradient-ascent model inversion reconstructs class-representative inputs from a trained image classifier; (2) MAP attribute inference recovers a sensitive tabular attribute from a released model.

raw.summary: Two ML-privacy attacks from scratch: (1) gradient-ascent model inversion reconstructs class-representative inputs from a trained image classifier; (2) MAP attribute inference recovers a sensitive tabular attribute from a released model.

projects.name: LLM training-data memorization (canary exposure)
projects.summary: Tiny char-LM trained on synthetic logs with 4 inserted canaries (x16). Mean canary exposure 19.9/33.2 bits; 4 secrets effectively memorized (>50% of max exposure). Control (never-inserted) exposure 1.1 bits.

raw.summary: Tiny char-LM trained on synthetic logs with 4 inserted canaries (x16). Mean canary exposure 19.9/33.2 bits; 4 secrets effectively memorized (>50% of max exposure). Control (never-inserted) exposure 1.1 bits.
raw.method: Secret Sharer canary exposure (perplexity-based membership test)

projects.name: CAPSTONE · CI-gated LLM AppSec red-team pipeline
projects.summary: CI-gated LLM AppSec red-team (full-suite): vulnerable target ASR 100% (gate FAIL), remediated target ASR 0% (gate PASS); threshold 0%. Reuses p2+p3 harnesses vs p4, fixes via p7.

raw.summary: CI-gated LLM AppSec red-team (full-suite): vulnerable target ASR 100% (gate FAIL), remediated target ASR 0% (gate PASS); threshold 0%. Reuses p2+p3 harnesses vs p4, fixes via p7.

projects.name: OWASP LLM Top 10 — threat lab & repo map
projects.summary: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) mapped to the repo's LLM-security projects.

raw.summary: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025) mapped to the repo's LLM-security projects.

projects.name: garak-style LLM scan (offline by default, real garak optional)
projects.summary: Offline garak-style red-team scan: 5 probes / 15 attempts vs the p4 RAG mock; overall ASR 100% (95% CI 100%-100%). Same parser ingests a real garak report.jsonl.

raw.summary: Offline garak-style red-team scan: 5 probes / 15 attempts vs the p4 RAG mock; overall ASR 100% (95% CI 100%-100%). Same parser ingests a real garak report.jsonl.

projects.name: promptfoo red team (OWASP-LLM, eval-as-code)
projects.summary: OWASP-LLM red team (offline pure-python harness, promptfoo-style) vs the p4 vulnerable RAG: 9/9 attacks landed (100%), 0 benign false-positives.

raw.summary: OWASP-LLM red team (offline pure-python harness, promptfoo-style) vs the p4 vulnerable RAG: 9/9 attacks landed (100%), 0 benign false-positives.
raw.harness: pure-python (offline default); promptfoo owasp:llm preset = optional path

projects.name: Vulnerable RAG (the authorized lab target)
projects.summary: Deliberately-vulnerable local RAG lab target (mock LLM, TF-IDF retriever) with 4/4 planted weaknesses reachable offline. Imported by p5/p6/p7.

raw.summary: Deliberately-vulnerable local RAG lab target (mock LLM, TF-IDF retriever) with 4/4 planted weaknesses reachable offline. Imported by p5/p6/p7.

projects.name: Attack the RAG (prompt injection & exfiltration)
projects.summary: Prompt-injection + exfiltration attacks vs the p4 lab RAG (offline mock).

raw.summary: Prompt-injection + exfiltration attacks vs the p4 lab RAG (offline mock).
raw.note: Offline deterministic mock target. Real garak/PyRIT + LLM via .env (optional).

projects.name: Agent tool-abuse (confused deputy) + a tool-call guardrail
projects.summary: Confused-deputy injections drove the agent to an unsafe tool call in 67% of episodes; the tool-call guardrail reduced that to 0% while leaving benign tasks working.

raw.summary: Confused-deputy injections drove the agent to an unsafe tool call in 67% of episodes; the tool-call guardrail reduced that to 0% while leaving benign tasks working.

projects.name: Defend the RAG (layered guardrails + from-scratch injection detector)
projects.summary: Layered guardrails cut prompt-injection ASR from 100% to 0% across 7 attacks; the from-scratch TF-IDF+LogReg detector scores ROC-AUC 1.000.

raw.summary: Layered guardrails cut prompt-injection ASR from 100% to 0% across 7 attacks; the from-scratch TF-IDF+LogReg detector scores ROC-AUC 1.000.

projects.name: Refusal direction (mechanistic interp of "abliteration")
projects.summary: Synthetic-activation simulation of the refusal-direction (abliteration) method: difference-in-means recovers a planted refusal axis (|cos|=0.98); inference-time directional ablation drops the refusal rate from 100% to 0% while capability stays at 62% (vs 62%). Analysis only; no modified weights are produced.

raw.summary: Synthetic-activation simulation of the refusal-direction (abliteration) method: difference-in-means recovers a planted refusal axis (|cos|=0.98); inference-time directional ablation drops the refusal rate from 100% to 0% while capability stays at 62% (vs 62%). Analysis only; no modified weights are produced.

projects.name: secure-ml-pipeline · pickle PoC → safetensors + scan + sign + CI gate
projects.summary: Trained a TinyMLP, shipped it as safetensors (round-trip OK). The opcode scanner flagged the pickle PoC as MALICIOUS (2 dangerous opcodes) while the safetensors artifact scanned clean. The sign/verify gate is correct: genuine PASS, tampered+unsigned BLOCKED.

raw.summary: Trained a TinyMLP, shipped it as safetensors (round-trip OK). The opcode scanner flagged the pickle PoC as MALICIOUS (2 dangerous opcodes) while the safetensors artifact scanned clean. The sign/verify gate is correct: genuine PASS, tampered+unsigned BLOCKED.

highlights.title: FGSM breaks a 99% MNIST classifier
highlights.label: accuracy
highlights.blurb: A tiny imperceptible perturbation collapses accuracy.

highlights.title: Guardrails kill RAG prompt-injection
highlights.label: attack success
highlights.blurb: Layered guardrails + a from-scratch injection detector.

highlights.title: Harden your own IDS against evasion
highlights.label: evasion rate
highlights.blurb: Constrained adversarial flows, then adversarial training.

highlights.title: Refusal lives on one axis (abliteration)
highlights.label: refusal rate
highlights.blurb: Ablating one direction removes refusals; capability retained 99%.

roadmap.title: Wire in real datasets
roadmap.detail: Swap synthetic fallbacks for EMBER / CICIDS / NSL-KDD / LANL on the detection track and re-measure.

roadmap.title: Run against real LLMs
roadmap.detail: Drop your API key (or local Ollama) into .env and re-run the LLM-security attacks/defenses on a real model.

roadmap.title: Abliterate a real open-weight model
roadmap.detail: Enable the p8 real-model path (transformers + a small Qwen/Llama) on a free Colab/Kaggle GPU.

roadmap.title: Ship the CI-gated red-team
roadmap.detail: Turn on the appsec-ci GitHub Action so every commit runs a red-team and fails on ASR regressions.

roadmap.title: Deploy this dashboard
roadmap.detail: Publish to GitHub Pages for a shareable 'click this link' portfolio artifact.

roadmap.title: Deepen a flagship
roadmap.detail: Pick one capstone, harden it to interview depth, and write the threat-model report.
